steps.title: Find the original mean
steps.content:
Add the five original counts from the table, then divide by $5$:
$$149+144+148+136+139=716$$
$$\frac{716}{5}=143.2$$

steps.title: Find the new mean
steps.content:
Add the sixth nest with $121$ eggs, then divide by $6$:
$$716+121=837$$
$$\frac{837}{6}=139.5$$

steps.title: Compare the means
steps.content:
The original mean is greater because
$$143.2>139.5$$
So the original data set has a greater mean than the new data set. The choices saying less than, equal, or not enough information do not match this comparison.
The answer is A.
